name: Tempura Onion Rings
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Salt, plus more for sprinkling
Lemon wedges, ketchup and Soy sauce, for serving
recipeInstructions:
1. In a deep fryer or large saucepan, heat the oil to 350°F. Meanwhile, put 1 cup of the cornstarch in a brown paper bag. Add the onion slices and shake well to coat. Transfer the coated onion rings to a large baking sheet.
2. In a large bowl, mix the remaining 1/2 cup cornstarch with the flour and 1 teaspoon salt. Slowly add 1-1/2 cups of cold water into the flour mixture while stirring vigorously with chopsticks or a fork. Ensure the batter is smooth and free of lumps, incorporating pieces of ice no larger than a nickel.
3. When the oil reaches the correct temperature, carefully add as many coated onion rings to the hot oil as will fit comfortably in the fryer (you may need to work in batches). Using chopsticks or a large fork, gently lift the rings into the hot oil and separate any that are sticking together. Fry the onion rings until they are golden brown all over, approximately 3 minutes. Remove the onion rings from the oil using a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels or a paper bag to remove excess oil. Repeat with the remaining onion rings and batter. If desired, sprinkle the onion rings with salt before serving.
4. Serve hot, warm, or at room temperature alongside lemon wedges, ketchup, and soy sauce. Serves 4 to 6.
